sections.summary: Document title: Standard for Tilsiter.
Standard identifier: CXS 270-1968.

sections.section: Formerly CODEX STAN C-11-1968. Adopted in 1968. Revised in 2007. Amended in 2008, 2010, 2013, 2018, 2019, 2022.
sections.summary: Document history noting adoption in 1968, revision in 2007 and amendments in 2008, 2010, 2013, 2018, 2019 and 2022.

2022 amendment: replaces the previous Section 7.5 wording on labelling of non-retail containers with a provision that labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021), and records this textual change.
This standard applies to Tilsiter cheese intended for direct consumption or for further processing in accordance with the product description in Section 2.

Defines Tilsiter as a ripened firm/semi-hard cheese with near white to yellow colour, firm texture suitable for cutting and irregular gas holes, manufactured with or without a well-dried smear-developed rind; typical ripening for consumption is about 3 weeks at 10–16°C but alternative ripening (including ripening enzymes) is allowed if the cheese exhibits similar physical, biochemical and sensory properties, while Tilsiter intended for further processing may be less ripened when justified by technical or trade needs.

Specifies essential composition and quality factors: raw materials limited to cows' and/or buffaloes' milk and derived products; permitted ingredients include harmless starter cultures and micro-organisms, rennet/coagulating enzymes, sodium/potassium chloride, potable water, ripening enzymes, processing aids and certain flours/starches as surface anti-caking agents under GMP; and sets compositional minima/maxima for milk fat and dry matter (detailed in Section 3.3) with deviations considered non-compliant with CXS 206-1999.
Permits only cows' milk, buffaloes' milk or mixtures thereof and products derived from these milks as raw materials for Tilsiter.

Lists permitted ingredients for Tilsiter including harmless lactic/flavour starter cultures and other harmless micro-organisms, rennet or other suitable coagulating enzymes, sodium or potassium chloride, potable water, safe ripening-enhancing enzymes, processing aids, and rice/corn/potato flours or starches restricted to surface anti-caking use on cut/sliced/shredded products only in amounts necessary under good manufacturing practice.

Requires milk fat in dry matter of at least 30% (no upper limit) with a reference range of 45–55%, and specifies minimum dry matter contents that vary by fat-in-dry-matter bands (e.g. 49% for 30–<40% fat, 53% for 40–<45%, 55% for 45–<50%, 57% for 50–<60%, 61% for 60–<85%), and states that compositional modifications beyond these minima/maxima are non-compliant with Section 4.3.3 of CXS 206-1999.

Only additive functional classes shown as justified in the table may be used: colours and acidity regulators are allowed in the cheese mass, preservatives allowed in cheese mass and surface/rind, and anti-caking agents are justified for surface/rind treatment in accordance with the referenced General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) and relevant tables.

Products covered by this standard must comply with maximum contaminant and toxin levels set in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995), and the milk used must meet the contaminant, veterinary drug residue and pesticide maximum limits established by the CAC for milk.

Recommends preparing and handling the product in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts, and complying with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

Adds specific labelling provisions to general Codex requirements: the name Tilsiter may be used only if the product conforms to this standard and may require qualification when fat content is outside the reference range; the country of origin must be declared with specified rules for substantial transformation; milk fat content must be declared in an acceptable format; date of manufacture may substitute minimum durability in certain non-retail cases; and non-retail containers must follow the relevant Codex standard.

Permits the name 'Tilsiter' only for cheeses that conform to this standard (alternative spellings allowed where customary), requires appropriate qualification if fat content falls outside the reference range but above the absolute minimum, and allows the designation also for cut, sliced, shredded or grated products made from conforming cheese.

Requires declaration of the country of origin defined as the country of manufacture, and where substantial transformation occurs in a second country that country is to be declared as the country of origin for labelling.

Requires milk fat content to be declared in a manner acceptable in the country of retail sale, either as percentage by mass, percentage of fat in dry matter, or grams per serving with number of servings stated.
Allows the date of manufacture to be declared instead of minimum durability information provided the product is not intended to be purchased as such by the final consumer.
States that labelling of non-retail containers should conform to the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Specifies that methods of sampling and analysis for checking compliance with the standard shall be those in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the standard's provisions.
